recipes.title: Spaghetti Carbonara
recipes.description: A proper Roman carbonara with no cream and no shortcuts. Guanciale, eggs, Pecorino, and patient stirring create a glossy sauce without scrambling.
instructions: Cook the spaghetti in heavily salted water until al dente. Reserve 200 ml of pasta water before draining.
Cut the guanciale into thick strips and cook it in a dry pan over medium-high heat until golden and crisp. Turn off the heat and let the pan cool slightly.
Whisk the eggs, egg yolks, finely grated Pecorino Romano, and Parmigiano Reggiano in a bowl. Season generously with freshly ground black pepper.
Add the hot pasta to the pan with the guanciale, away from the heat. Add the egg mixture and stir quickly while adding pasta water a little at a time until the sauce is silky and creamy.
Serve immediately on warm plates with extra Pecorino and a final grind of black pepper.

recipes.title: Butter-Braised Lamb Shanks with Red Wine
recipes.description: A slow-cooked dinner that rewards patience. The lamb braises for hours until it falls from the bone and the sauce turns deep, glossy, and rich.
instructions: Heat the oven to 160 C. Season the lamb shanks generously with salt and pepper on all sides.
Brown the shanks in olive oil in a heavy pot over high heat for 3 to 4 minutes per side, then set them aside.
Lower the heat to medium and cook chopped onions, carrots, and whole garlic cloves in the same pot for 8 minutes. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Add rosemary, thyme, and red wine. Scrape up the browned bits from the bottom of the pot and reduce the wine by half.
Return the shanks to the pot, cover, and braise in the oven for 2.5 to 3 hours, turning once. The meat is ready when it falls from the bone.
Remove the shanks and reduce the sauce on the stove until thick and shiny. Season to taste and serve with mashed potatoes or polenta.

recipes.title: Rustic Sourdough Bread
recipes.description: A two-day bread with very little active work. The long cold proof develops a gentle tang, an open crumb, and a crisp caramelized crust.
instructions: On Friday evening, mix wheat flour, rye flour, lukewarm water, and active sourdough starter in a bowl. Rest for 30 minutes.
Add salt dissolved in a little water. Fold the dough by stretching it up and over itself, repeating several times over 2 hours.
Shape the dough gently into a round without knocking out the air. Place it in a floured proofing basket or towel-lined bowl and refrigerate overnight.
On Saturday morning, heat the oven to 250 C with a cast-iron pot inside for at least 45 minutes.
Turn the cold dough into the hot pot, score the top, bake covered for 20 minutes, then uncover and bake for another 20 to 25 minutes.
Cool the bread on a rack for at least 1 hour before slicing so the crumb can set.

recipes.title: Pan-Fried Cod Loins with Beurre Blanc
recipes.description: A simple dish built around good ingredients: golden pan-fried cod loins with a classic French butter sauce.
instructions: Take the cod out of the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking. Pat it dry and season with salt.
For the beurre blanc, reduce white wine, finely chopped shallots, and lemon juice in a small pan until almost dry. Add cream and reduce by half.
Whisk cold butter into the warm reduction a few cubes at a time until the sauce is glossy and emulsified. Season with salt and lemon juice.
Heat olive oil in a pan until very hot. Add the cod presentation-side down and press gently for the first 10 seconds.
Cook for 4 to 5 minutes until golden, then turn carefully and cook for 1 to 2 minutes more.
Serve the cod golden-side up and spoon the beurre blanc over it at the table.

recipes.title: Chicken Katsu Curry
recipes.description: Japanese comfort food: crisp panko-coated chicken over steamed rice with a thick, gently sweet curry sauce.
instructions: Cook the curry sauce by softening onion and carrot in butter, adding Japanese curry roux and water, and simmering until thick.
Pound the chicken breasts flat and season with salt and pepper.
Bread the chicken in flour, beaten egg, and panko. Press the panko firmly onto the meat.
Fry the chicken in plenty of oil at 170 C for 5 to 6 minutes per side until deeply golden and cooked through.
Rest the chicken on a rack for 2 minutes, then slice it thickly on the diagonal.
Serve over steamed rice with curry sauce on the side so the breading stays crisp.

recipes.title: Tacos al Pastor
recipes.description: Iconic Mexico City street food with thin slices of pork marinated in achiote and chili, grilled until caramelized, and served with pineapple.
instructions: Blend achiote paste, chipotle in adobo, garlic, orange juice, vinegar, and spices into a smooth marinade.
Slice the pork thinly, coat it in the marinade, cover, and marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat a grill pan or grill until very hot and cook the pork for 2 to 3 minutes per side until caramelized.
Grill pineapple slices until marked, then dice them.
Warm the tortillas directly over a flame or in a dry pan until soft and lightly charred.
Assemble tacos with pork, pineapple, finely chopped onion, cilantro, and a generous squeeze of lime.

recipes.title: Butter Chicken
recipes.description: A creamy Delhi classic with spiced yogurt-marinated chicken folded into a silky tomato, butter, and cream sauce.
instructions: Marinate the chicken thighs in yogurt, garam masala, chili, ginger, and garlic for at least 1 hour, preferably 4.
Grill or sear the chicken over high heat for 5 to 6 minutes per side until lightly charred. Set aside.
For the sauce, cook onion, ginger, and garlic in butter until golden. Add canned tomatoes and garam masala and simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend the sauce until smooth, then strain it back into the pot for a silky texture.
Add cream and butter, then simmer the chicken in the sauce for 10 minutes until thick and well coated.
Season with salt and serve with naan or basmati rice.

recipes.title: Lamb Tagine with Dates and Almonds
recipes.description: Moroccan slow cooking with tender lamb, sweet dates, toasted almonds, and a fragrant sauce of saffron, cinnamon, and cumin.
instructions: Cut the lamb shoulder into large cubes. Season with salt, cumin, and cinnamon, then brown it in batches in olive oil.
Cook onion and garlic in the same pot until soft. Add saffron bloomed in warm water, the remaining spices, and tomato paste.
Return the lamb to the pot, add water, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook gently for 1.5 hours.
Add dates and drained chickpeas and simmer for another 30 minutes until the meat is tender and the sauce is thick.
Toast almonds in a dry pan until golden.
Serve the tagine with toasted almonds and fresh cilantro, with couscous or bread on the side.

recipes.title: Pad Thai
recipes.description: Bangkok-style rice noodles tossed in a balanced tamarind, fish sauce, and sugar sauce with shrimp, peanuts, sprouts, and lime.
instructions: Soak the rice noodles in warm water for 15 to 20 minutes until flexible but still firm, then drain.
Mix tamarind paste, fish sauce, sugar, and water until the sugar dissolves.
Heat a wok until smoking. Cook the shrimp for 1 minute and set aside. Add oil and beaten eggs, then stir into large curds and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok, then add the noodles and sauce. Toss for 2 to 3 minutes until the noodles absorb the sauce and soften.
Add shrimp, eggs, half the bean sprouts, and scallions. Toss quickly to combine.
Serve with roasted peanuts, the remaining sprouts, lime wedges, and fresh cilantro.

recipes.title: Cacio e Pepe
recipes.description: A minimalist Roman pasta classic made with Pecorino Romano, black pepper, and starchy pasta water. Simple ingredients, but the technique creates a glossy, creamy sauce without cream.
instructions: Cook the pasta in heavily salted water until al dente. Reserve pasta water before draining.
Toast freshly cracked black pepper in a dry pan until fragrant.
Add a splash of pasta water to the pan and let it bubble with the pepper.
Add the pasta and toss quickly while adding grated Pecorino little by little.
Keep tossing and adding pasta water until the cheese emulsifies into a smooth sauce.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and black pepper.

recipes.title: Pasta Amatriciana
recipes.description: A Roman pasta classic with guanciale, tomato, Pecorino Romano, and chili. Rich, salty, sharp, and deeply satisfying.
instructions: Cut guanciale into strips and cook slowly until the fat renders and the meat becomes crisp.
Add chili flakes and cook briefly.
Add crushed tomatoes and simmer until thickened.
Cook the pasta until al dente and reserve pasta water.
Toss the pasta with the sauce, adding pasta water as needed.
Finish with grated Pecorino Romano and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Lasagna alla Bolognese
recipes.description: A layered Italian classic with slow-cooked meat ragu, creamy bechamel, pasta sheets, and Parmigiano baked until golden.
instructions: Cook onion, carrot, and celery in olive oil until softened.
Add minced beef and pork and brown thoroughly.
Add tomato paste, canned tomatoes, and milk, then simmer the ragu until rich.
Make a bechamel with butter, flour, and milk.
Layer ragu, bechamel, pasta sheets, and Parmigiano in a baking dish.
Bake until bubbling and golden, then rest before slicing.

recipes.title: Boeuf Bourguignon
recipes.description: A deep, slow-cooked French beef stew with red wine, mushrooms, onions, carrots, and herbs. Rich, glossy, and built for cold evenings.
instructions: Cut beef into large cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Brown the beef in batches in a heavy pot until deeply caramelized.
Cook bacon, onions, carrots, and garlic in the same pot.
Add tomato paste and cook briefly.
Add red wine, beef stock, thyme, and bay leaf, then return the beef.
Braise gently until tender, adding mushrooms near the end.

recipes.title: French Onion Soup
recipes.description: A classic French soup made from deeply caramelized onions, beef stock, toasted bread, and a bubbling layer of melted cheese.
instructions: Slice the onions thinly.
Cook the onions slowly in butter until deeply golden and caramelized.
Add flour and stir for 1 minute.
Add white wine and reduce slightly.
Add beef stock and simmer until rich and sweet.
Top with toasted bread and cheese, then broil until bubbling.

recipes.title: Tonkotsu Ramen
recipes.description: A Japanese ramen classic with rich pork broth, noodles, chashu pork, soft-boiled egg, scallions, and aromatic toppings.
instructions: Prepare or heat a rich pork broth.
Cook ramen noodles according to package instructions.
Warm sliced chashu pork gently in broth or sauce.
Prepare soft-boiled eggs and slice scallions.
Add tare to the bowl, then ladle in hot broth.
Add noodles, pork, egg, scallions, and toppings before serving.

recipes.title: Gyoza
recipes.description: Japanese pan-fried dumplings filled with pork, cabbage, garlic, ginger, and scallions. Crisp on the bottom, juicy inside.
instructions: Finely chop cabbage and squeeze out excess moisture.
Mix pork, cabbage, garlic, ginger, scallions, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Place filling in each wrapper and fold tightly.
Fry the dumplings in oil until the bottoms are golden.
Add water, cover, and steam until cooked through.
Uncover and crisp the bottoms again before serving.

recipes.title: Palak Paneer
recipes.description: A North Indian classic with paneer cubes folded into a smooth spinach sauce flavored with garlic, ginger, spices, and cream.
instructions: Blanch spinach briefly, then cool and blend into a smooth puree.
Cook onion, garlic, ginger, and green chili in oil or ghee.
Add spices and cook until fragrant.
Add the spinach puree and simmer gently.
Add paneer cubes and cook until warmed through.
Finish with cream and serve with naan or rice.

recipes.title: Chicken Biryani
recipes.description: A layered Indian rice dish with marinated chicken, basmati rice, fried onions, saffron, herbs, and warm spices.
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, garlic, ginger, chili, and biryani spices.
Rinse and partially cook basmati rice until just underdone.
Fry onions until golden and crisp.
Cook the marinated chicken until partly done.
Layer chicken, rice, fried onions, herbs, and saffron milk in a heavy pot.
Cover tightly and steam on low heat until the rice is fluffy and the chicken is tender.

recipes.title: Thai Green Curry
recipes.description: A Thai classic with green curry paste, coconut milk, chicken, vegetables, fish sauce, basil, and lime. Fragrant, spicy, and creamy.
instructions: Fry green curry paste in thick coconut milk until fragrant.
Add chicken and coat it in the curry paste.
Add the remaining coconut milk and simmer gently.
Add vegetables and cook until just tender.
Season with fish sauce, sugar, and lime.
Finish with Thai basil and serve with jasmine rice.

recipes.title: Crispy Pork Belly with Parsley Sauce
recipes.description: One of Denmark's most iconic dishes: crispy slices of pork belly served with boiled potatoes and creamy parsley sauce.
instructions: Season the pork belly slices with salt.
Roast or fry the slices until deeply crisp and golden.
Boil potatoes in salted water until tender.
Make a white sauce by whisking butter, flour, and milk together.
Stir chopped parsley into the sauce and season with salt and pepper.
Serve the crispy pork with potatoes and parsley sauce.
